title: Webhook key in URL query string leaks credential to logs and referrer headers
description:
In src/webhook.rs the authentication key is accepted as a URL query parameter (?key=...). This means the secret appears in:
- Reverse proxy access logs (nginx, caddy, haproxy all log the full URI by default)
- HTTP Referer header on any redirect the handler or upstream follows
- Browser history if any human ever tests the URL

The key never rotates automatically. A log compromise retroactively exposes all past keys for all personas.

Mitigation: move the key to an Authorization header (Bearer) or X-Webhook-Key header. Headers are excluded from access log URI fields by convention and are never sent as Referer.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
